title: web.fetch (v1) — RPC request
description:
Page-fetch request for the web-fetcher Unix-socket RPC. Filed 2026-05-10 with v0.45 §6.8 — sibling to `web.search.v1`. Caller hands a URL; the agent fetches it, runs Mozilla Readability to extract main-article plaintext, and returns the result with provenance.

Transport: Unix socket at `/var/run/ai-web-fetcher/query.sock`. Caller authenticated via SO_PEERCRED.

v1 backend: plain HTTP GET (`User-Agent: ai-web-fetcher/<version>`) + Mozilla Readability. No headless browser — JS-rendered SPAs degrade to 'low_signal' in the response and the consumer treats them as `(external lookup unavailable for X)` per AP-1.

Allowlist gate: the URL's hostname MUST resolve to an entry on `registry/web-allowlist.yaml`. Off-allowlist hostnames return `not_allowlisted` — fail loud, never silent fall-through (AP-1). The receiver enforces the per-day / per-task / per-month spend caps + the per-fetch byte cap (`WEB_FETCH_MAX_BYTES=200000`) and timeout (`WEB_FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS=8000`).

Prompt-injection mitigations: the receiver returns plaintext only (never raw HTML); the consumer is expected to wrap returned text in `<UNTRUSTED_WEB origin sha256>` delimiters before any LLM ingestion. Output post-filter on the consumer side drops URLs not in the per-fetch provenance set. Per-fetch provenance row is written to ops.db `events` (kind: `web.fetch.completed`) with sha256 of fetched bytes for audit.

Status: draft. Do not flip to active until web-fetcher implementation ships and its boot self-check passes once on `golden-ai-ops`.

contract_id.description: Contract identifier. Must be the literal string 'web.fetch.v1'.

trace_id.description: UUIDv7. Logged in the per-fetch provenance row.

dedupe_key.description: SHA256 of '<caller_agent_id>:web.fetch.v1:<url_canonical>:<freshness>'. Cache hit short-circuits to the cached row when freshness != 'realtime'.

source_ref.description: URL or ID of the caller context that triggered this fetch (e.g. a task UUID, a Runs row id). Stored in the provenance row for audit.

caller_agent_id.description: Self-asserted agent ID of the caller. Authorisation decisions key off SO_PEERCRED uid.

url.description: Absolute http(s) URL to fetch. Hostname MUST resolve to an entry on `registry/web-allowlist.yaml`; off-allowlist returns `not_allowlisted`. Fragment identifiers are stripped before fetch (cache key is hostname + path + query).

freshness.description: Cache policy. `cached` returns the existing row if it's within the per-domain TTL (TTL_INSURANCE_MED_S=86400, TTL_AIRLINE_S=3600, TTL_GENERIC_S=604800 — set by hostname pattern in the agent's config). `realtime` bypasses the cache and forces a re-fetch; reserved for `ai-doer` runtime fetches that cannot tolerate ~1d staleness.

max_bytes.description: Optional per-call body cap, capped by `WEB_FETCH_MAX_BYTES=200000` (the agent's hard ceiling). Larger pages are truncated mid-stream and the response is flagged `truncated: true` so the consumer knows the plaintext is partial.
